_note: John Deere MODELS MASTER (equipment fitment index), harvested LIVE from shop.deere.com on 2026-06-18. Every John Deere MODEL with at least one seal-family part, plus per-category counts (oring/seal/gasket/backup). Equipment/Make/Model layer: customer names their model, Marion confirms it's a known JD model and how many parts exist per category, then builds the part list ON DEMAND via the live fitment query (recipe below). Make is always 'John Deere'; Serial handled at query time.

on_demand_fitment_recipe.why: Part<->model links NOT pre-stored (would be ~millions-row matrix). Build live when a customer gives a model.
required_params: s (Constructor.io creds from a live shop.deere.com page/beacon)
on_demand_fitment_recipe.note: 10k cap applies per (model,category) query but per-model counts are far below 10k, so per-model fitment pulls are COMPLETE.

serial_note: shop.deere.com filters by MODEL, not serial. Serial-break/PIN-level fitment lives behind partscatalog.deere.com (login+serial gated), NOT in this dataset. For Marion: capture serial as free-text, flag serial-dependent variants, resolve precise fitment via JD parts catalog or dealer.

DATA_INTEGRITY_NOTE.facet_cap_2000: Each category fitment facet returns at most 2,000 model options. Union of 4 categories = 3,669 unique models. A few models never ranking in any category top 2,000 may be missing. Common/heavy-hitter models all present.
DATA_INTEGRITY_NOTE.model_string_only: Models are raw JD facet strings (e.g. '8400','310 P','8R 340','S690','LX188'). No normalization.
